column3: Preparatory meeting for logistics needs assessment for all pillars.

Vehicle fleet identification: Ambulances 4 (HGR Bunia, HGR Mongbwalu, PNC and UMIR). Programmes & Coordinations: 3 (CPLT, MTN, PEV). WHO: 5 vehicles for surveillance pillar. UNICEF: 6 rented vehicles including 1 for SBC.

Development of logistics needs assessment for HZ Goma, Katwa, Beni, Karisimbi, Mutwanga, Vuhovi, Kalunguta, Kamango, Mabalako, Oicha, Museniene, Kayina, Alimbongo, Butembo. Acquisition of Starlink kits for internet connection from UNICEF. Receipt of 5 vehicles donated by WHO. Receipt of 200 litres of diesel (MEMISA-Belgium support).

Delivery of inputs and protective equipment to laboratory and IPC pillars for 4 category 1 facilities (HGR Bunia, HGR Rwampara, CH Salama, CH CME). Delivery of inputs and protective equipment to Lita HZ for SDB. Unpacking of inputs and equipment received on 21 May 2026. Monitoring of installation works for care tents at HGR Bunia (ALIMA and WHO) and ETC installation at Mongbwalu by MSF.
Monitoring of ETC construction works at HGR Bunia (finishing works under way), CME Bunia, HGR Rwampara (ETC); monitoring of ETC rehabilitation works at HGR Katwa.

Delivery of laboratory materials to INRB/Beni, incl. 24 thermo-flash for the PNHF and 4 thermo-flash for HGR Katwa via Katwa HZ; preparation of the supply needs assessment for the pillars in Katwa HZ; monitoring of zone-vehicle maintenance and movement of the 4 vehicles provided by IOM.

Monitoring of ETC construction works at HGR Bunia (finishing works under way), CME Bunia, HGR Rwampara (ETC). Delivery of IPC inputs to CS Bigo, CME Bunia (crèche), DPS and CHEM-CHEM orphanage. Delivery of medicines in Bunia HZ. Monitoring of ETC rehabilitation works at HGR Katwa. Continued support to regular needs of pillars.

Supply of 31 health facilities/health zones with inputs and 5 health zones with medicines (critical items). 135 motorcycles ensure the mobility of field teams at the level of various health zones and pillars. 34 vehicles (2 functional ambulances) ensure the mobility of coordination and field teams in the various health zones.
